howTo.title: How to Use HourWise EU
howTo.subtitle: Everything you need to know to get started with fleet management

step1.title: Step 1: Create Your Account
step1.description: Start by creating a fleet manager account. You'll need to provide your company information and create a secure password. This account will give you full access to manage your fleet.

step2.title: Step 2: Add Your Drivers
step2.description: Once logged in, navigate to the Driver Management section. You can add drivers by creating accounts for them. Each driver will receive credentials to log in and submit their daily hours.
list: Enter driver name and email address
Driver receives welcome email with instructions

step3.title: Step 3: Track Daily Hours
step3.description: Drivers log in to their dashboard to submit daily hours. The system automatically tracks their cumulative hours and alerts them when approaching regulatory limits.
list: Real-time calculation of weekly and fortnightly totals

compliance.title: Compliance Monitoring
compliance.description: View real-time compliance status for your entire fleet. The Compliance Scoreboard shows which drivers are within limits and which require attention. Click on any driver to see detailed hour breakdowns.

violations.title: Violation Management
violations.description: When a driver exceeds regulatory limits, the system automatically logs an infraction. View all infractions in the Infraction Report, where you can see violation details, dates, and driver information for audit purposes.

audit.title: Audit Trail
audit.description: Every action in the system is logged in the Audit Trail. Track when drivers were added, hours were submitted, or settings were changed. This comprehensive log ensures complete accountability and meets regulatory requirements.

step1.title: Log in to your driver dashboard
step1.description: Use the credentials provided by your fleet manager

step2.title: Submit your daily driving hours
step2.description: Enter the total hours driven for the day

step3.title: Check your compliance status
step3.description: View your weekly and fortnightly hour totals

step4.title: Pay attention to warnings
step4.description: The system alerts you when approaching hour limits

limits.title: Key Limits
limits.weeklyValue: Maximum 56 hours of driving per week
limits.fortnightlyValue: Maximum 90 hours over any two consecutive weeks
limits.footnote: HourWise EU automatically monitors both limits and alerts drivers and managers when approaching or exceeding these thresholds.

help.title: Need More Help?
help.subtitle: Contact our support team for personalized assistance

intro.title: 1. Introduction
intro.p1: HourWise EU ("we", "our", or "us") provides a fleet management platform designed to help transport businesses ensure compliance and manage their operations. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, and safeguard the information of Fleet Managers and their associated Drivers when using our platform.
intro.p2: This policy applies to both our web portal (the "Portal") and our mobile application (the "App"). By using our services, you agree to the collection and use of information in accordance with this policy.

managers.title: 2.1 Information Provided by Fleet Managers
items: Manager Account Information: Full name, company name, and email address.
Driver Records: Managers enter driver details including Payroll Number, Date of Birth, National Insurance Number, Address, and Emergency Contact details.
Compliance Details: Driving Licence, CPC/DQC numbers, and expiry dates.

drivers.title: 2.2 Information Provided by Drivers (via the App)
items: Profile Data: Full name and email address used for account creation.
Work Sessions: Timestamps for work, break, and rest periods.
Expense Records: Amount, description, and images of receipts.

app.title: 2.3 App Permissions & Data
app.description: To provide essential compliance and management features, the App requests the following permissions:
items: Camera Access: We require camera access to allow you to take and upload photos of your Driving Licence, CPC cards, and fuel/expense receipts directly to the portal for compliance and reimbursement.

Location Data: We capture a single GPS coordinate "stamp" only at the exact moment you Start and End a shift. This is used to verify work locations for fleet audit purposes and is not used for continuous background tracking.

use.title: 3. How We Use Your Information
items: Compliance Tracking: Managing driver hours and tracking document expiries (Licence/CPC).
Payroll Facilitation: Calculating gross pay and generating summaries for employer accounting.
Expense Management: Processing driver-submitted receipts for business reconciliation.
Audit Trails: Maintaining geographic and temporal records of work sessions for legal compliance.

security.title: 4. Data Security & Storage
security.p1: All data is protected using Supabase Row Level Security (RLS), ensuring that users can only access data belonging to their own company. Documents are stored in encrypted, private storage buckets. Data is encrypted both at rest and in transit.

retention.title: 5. Data Retention
retention.p1: As required by UK and EU tax and labor laws, payroll-related "Records of Wages" and work sessions are retained for a minimum of 3 to 6 years (depending on jurisdiction) for audit purposes, even if an account is deactivated.

rights.title: 6. Your Data Rights (GDPR)
rights.p1: Under GDPR and the UK DPA, you have the right to access, rectify, or erase your data. Drivers may request data actions via their Fleet Manager or directly through our portal:

agreement.title: 1. Agreement to Terms
agreement.p1: By accessing or using the HourWise EU platform (the "Service"), provided by HourWise EU ("we", "us", or "our"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service ("Terms"). If you disagree with any part of the terms, you may not access the Service.

license.title: 2. Use License
license.p1: Permission is granted to temporarily use the HourWise EU Portal and Mobile App for business fleet management or individual professional driving purposes. This is the grant of a license, not a transfer of title, and under this license you may not:
items: Modify or copy the materials;
Use the materials for any commercial purpose other than managing your authorized fleet or individual driving records;
Attempt to decompile or reverse engineer any software contained in the Service;
Remove any copyright or other proprietary notations from the materials.

accuracy.title: 3. Accuracy of Data & Compliance
accuracy.p1: HourWise EU provides tools to assist with fleet compliance and payroll calculations. However:
items: User Responsibility: Users (Managers and Solo Drivers) are solely responsible for verifying the accuracy of driver logs, vehicle checks, and payroll summaries generated by the Service.

Legal Compliance: Use of the Service does not guarantee legal compliance with DVSA, EU Drivers' Hours, or other transport regulations. Users must ensure their operations meet all local and international laws.
Data Entry: We are not responsible for errors resulting from incorrect data entry by any user.

subscriptions.title: 4. Subscriptions and Billing
subscriptions.p1: Access to professional features requires a paid subscription. All billing is handled via our third-party processor (Stripe).

solo.title: 4.1 Solo Driver Plan
solo.p1: Individual drivers using the Service independently are billed on a simple monthly subscription basis. Billing is managed directly through the mobile application.

fleet.title: 4.2 Fleet Management Plan
fleet.p1: Transport companies are billed based on the number of active drivers linked to their fleet. The Fleet Manager is responsible for payment of all driver seats associated with their company account.

general.title: 4.3 General Billing Terms
items: Fees are non-refundable except as required by law.
Fleet drivers are covered by their company's subscription and do not require individual payments while linked to a fleet.
Failure to maintain a valid payment method may result in temporary suspension of Service access.

liability.title: 5. Disclaimer & Limitation of Liability
liability.p1: The Service is provided on an "AS IS" and "AS AVAILABLE" basis. To the maximum extent permitted by law, HourWise EU shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including without limitation, loss of profits, data, use, or other intangible losses, resulting from:
items: Your use or inability to use the Service;
Any unauthorized access to or use of our secure servers and/or any personal information stored therein;
Errors or omissions in any content or data.

law.title: 6. Governing Law
law.p1: These Terms shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the United Kingdom, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.

changes.title: 7. Changes to Terms
changes.p1: We reserve the right, at our sole discretion, to modify or replace these Terms at any time. We will provide at least 30 days' notice prior to any new terms taking effect for active subscribers.
